assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. The detailed findings (lines 49-58) show all five criteria individually marked PASS: Criterion 1 (single license filter), Criterion 2 (comma-separated filter), Criterion 3 (400 validation), Criterion 4 (pagination integration), Criterion 5 (unchanged response shape). The separate criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md files each state 'Verdict: PASS' with detailed reasoning.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR files exactly match the task specification: 2 modified files (`list.rs`, `service/mod.rs`) and 1 new file (`tests/api/package.rs`); no out-of-scope or unimplemented files'. The detailed findings (lines 27-30) list all three files with their modification types and confirm 'No out-of-scope files. No unimplemented files.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments exist on this PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. There is no mention of sub-task creation anywhere in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md — all are documentation/report files. The report.md content describes reading and analyzing the diff (e.g., 'All added lines were scanned across 3 files' at line 36) but shows no evidence of modifying code files. The criterion files contain analysis with code excerpts quoted for evidence, not code modifications.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' (line 17) and presents findings across all domains. There is no merge action, no gh pr merge command output, and no indication of auto-merging. The report footer (line 83) states '*This comment was AI-generated by [sdlc-workflow/verify-pr]*' indicating it was posted as a comment for human review.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive data detected in added lines across all 3 files'. The detailed findings (lines 36-44) enumerate the specific categories scanned (hardcoded passwords/secrets, API keys/tokens, private keys/certificates, environment/configuration files, cloud provider credentials, database credentials) and confirm 'No matches found'.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test file (`tests/api/package.rs`) with 4 new test functions and 80 lines; no test files modified or deleted'. The detailed finding (lines 79-80) states 'ADDITIVE: The only test file `tests/api/package.rs` is newly created (diff header: `new file mode 100644`). It adds 4 test functions and 80 lines of test code. No existing test files were modified or deleted.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md summary table (lines 5-15) contains all expected verdicts: Review Feedback (N/A), Root-Cause Investigation (N/A), Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (PASS) — Intent Alignment domain; Sensitive Patterns (PASS) — Security domain; CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Verification Commands (N/A) — Correctness domain; Test Quality (PASS, with sub-components: Repetitive Test Detection: PASS, Test Documentation: PASS, Eval Quality: N/A) and Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE) — Style/Conventions domain. All four domains are represented with their expected sub-verdicts.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: The 'Domain Findings' section (lines 23-80) provides: (1) Intent Alignment: file-by-file scope comparison listing all 3 files with paths and modification types (lines 27-30); (2) Security: line-level pattern scanning listing 6 categories scanned with 'No matches found' (lines 36-44); (3) Correctness: per-criterion code-level verification with code references like 'spdx::Expression::parse', 'is_in filter', 'AppError::BadRequest', SeaORM query construction details, and specific test assertions (lines 48-58); (4) Style/Conventions: test quality assessment listing each test function with its distinct setup, assertion targets, and doc comment patterns (lines 60-80). Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide even deeper per-criterion analysis with code-level evidence.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | Repetitive Test Detection: PASS (4 tests have meaningfully different setups and assertions, no parameterization candidates); Test Documentation: PASS (all 4 test functions have `///` doc comments); Eval Quality: N/A'. Line 76: 'Eval Quality -- N/A: No eval result reviews found on this PR.' The Test Quality combination is PASS based only on Repetitive Test Detection and Test Documentation, with Eval Quality explicitly N/A and not affecting the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 76: 'Eval Quality -- N/A: No eval result reviews found on this PR.'). No sub-tasks of any kind are mentioned in any output file. The Root-Cause Investigation row (line 6) states 'N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate', confirming no sub-tasks were created.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments exist on this PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. These appear at the top of the summary table (lines 5-6) before the domain-specific verdicts (lines 7-15), and their reasoning is self-contained ('No review comments exist', 'No sub-tasks were created') rather than referencing domain analysis outputs, indicating independent determination by the orchestrator.
